explanation: Whenever 'prop_0' becomes true, it should be followed by 'prop_1' at the same time or in the future. This is expressed by: globally, if 'prop_0' occurs, then eventually 'prop_1' must occur.

The statement 'If prop_0, prop_1' can be interpreted as: whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must also hold at that point. In LTL, this is expressed as globally (always), if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 is also true. This is written as G (prop_0 -> prop_1).

The phrase 'If prop_0, prop_1' implies that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true at the same moment. In LTL, this can be expressed globally: at all times, if prop_0 holds then prop_1 must also hold.

The phrase 'If prop_0, prop_1' expresses an implication: whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time. In LTL, this is expressed as 'globally, prop_0 implies prop_1', or G(prop_0 -> prop_1).

The statement 'If prop_0, prop_1' is equivalent to 'Whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 also holds', which is a safety property. In LTL, this is expressed as 'Globally, if prop_0 holds at any moment, then prop_1 must hold at the same moment.'
The statement requires that whenever prop_0 holds, then (in the future) prop_1 should also hold. In LTL, this is written as: Globally, if prop_0, then eventually prop_1.
This statement expresses that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time—an implication that always applies. In LTL, this is written as 'globally (prop_0 implies prop_1)'.

The natural language statement 'If prop_0, prop_1' can be read as 'whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 also holds.' This is captured by G (prop_0 -> prop_1): globally, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must also be true at that moment.
The phrase 'If prop_0, prop_1 and why' seems incomplete, but if we interpret it as 'If prop_0 holds, then prop_1 holds,' the standard LTL translation would be 'Globally, if prop_0 then prop_1.'
The sentence is incomplete, but interpreting it as 'If prop_0 then prop_1', this means whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time (globally).
